metadata.title: LABoR-AND THE BLACK HOLE OF CALCUTTA

pages.raw_text:
In these days when millions of men idle hear
and more about wages being fixed by competition "
There is great movement going on among the great
employers of the land to lower wages and they call the
process Competition"


When great crowd of men gather at the doors of the
machine shop the packing house the steel mill asking
in vain for work. and ready under the compuson of want
to take anything it is called 'competition in the labor
market


Surajah Dowlah, Hindoo prince in war with the
English put 146 prisoners in but Is feet square and
left them there. The prisoners Engishmen with the
Engishmans sporting blood and maniless and love of
fair play but when the air grew foul in this Black Hole Of
Calcutta it is called the prisoners begin crowdng to
the window at which air could be admitted They be-
gan competing idle laborers competing in the
labor market today. The pushing and crowdng toward
the window became frenzed and beastke fight in which
every human consideration lost sight Of They tore
at each other with tooth and nail Weak women went
down in the press of crazed human animals and heels
were stamped into their faces stood upon
them to get to the little window 1t the browning hor
of those horrible When the morning dawned,
only of the prisoners alive The rest of them
lay dead, stranged suocated, trampled down in the
"compeon of the air market of the Black Hole of Cal
cutta


Anyone see that this not competition, but the
really involuntary struggles of suffocatng for air.
1t competition than the grip of the drowning
is competition when he drags down his would be-
rescuers.


The socalled competition of the labor market is
unfair spurious brutal that Of the Black Hole
The employers have the only window at which
get either food clothing sheer-the window of
poymen which they ''give' men.


The knot of the problem lies in monopoly Of opporun-
ity for employmen Men cannot employ themselves.
Other the opportunities. Not the machinery and
the factory only If the face of nature from which
all employment not monopoized could build
their machines and factories and operate them
operatvey. But the planet which live is fenced up
and owned Our ancestors of only couple of generations
ago went into the forest out the prairie when work
got scarce, and employed themselves And had no
strikes, ockouts labor troubles. The unemployed
drained off into the great of unenced and
monopozed nature Men would not work for other men
except for high they could make working for
themselves America the land of industrial peace.
of good of refuge for the oppressed of all lands


Until opportunity for self-empoyment is again made
Free there is in talking about treating labor as
capital is treated. Laws must be made favorable
for labor than for capital if anything like square deal
is to prevail Would have been fair to make the same
rules and regulations for the and women in the Black
Hole as for those breathing the free air ouside Law
makers, writer's courts must remember that labor is in
the Black Hole of onesded competition and not in the
free atmosphere of unrestricted opportunity.
